what_it_does: A self-contained browser interaction binder (IIFE) that listens for gallery:rendered and decision:rendered events, walks a rendered container to find addressable content elements, attaches a small annotation UI strip (comment, reaction, favour) to each, and emits address-keyed direction items as gallery:verb (plus a legacy gallery:direction alias). It also exposes a decide() helper for recording decision-card takes, validating the decision address shape and refusing non-canonical addresses rather than silently missing.
observations: Implements a self-wiring IIFE that binds after gallery:rendered / decision:rendered CustomEvents.
Uses data-address / data-element-id and a dataset.viBound flag for idempotent per-element binding.
Emits a unified gallery:verb envelope with verb, aim_address, and payload, while also re-emitting the older gallery:direction shape as a transition alias.
Includes a comment picker rendered as a single panel with six content-type chips and a textarea.
Provides a decide() path that validates decision://<frame>/<id> shape and throws on non-canonical addresses.
Falls back to semantic HTML selectors (p, h1-h6, li, etc.) when no DNA-provided anchorableSelector or [data-anchorable] is present.
Warns loudly in the console when zero anchorable elements are found under a rendered unit.
Exposes window.galleryBinder for explicit DNA-driven binding.
standouts: The strict, loud refusal to emit decision takes for non-canonical decision addresses instead of silently missing.
Dual event emission (gallery:verb + legacy gallery:direction) as an intentional transition alias.
Listening to both gallery:rendered and decision:rendered so annotate binding reaches decision cards.
The zero-bind console warning surfaces a real integration failure mode rather than failing quietly.
conventions: IIFE module pattern with a small public window.galleryBinder API.
CustomEvent dispatch for decoupled communication between rendering, binding, and state layers.
Idempotent binding via dataset flags and early returns.
String-built innerHTML for UI fragments with constants from vocab arrays.
Semantic-HTML fallback selector when the producer does not declare its own anchorable set.
concerns: Uses window.prompt for favour input, which is blocking and offers poor UX.
Builds UI HTML by concatenating strings; while inputs are mostly constants, any future dynamic interpolation would risk injection.
markPlaced sets textContent then immediately overwrites it with innerHTML, which is redundant.
Re-emits every annotation as both gallery:verb and gallery:direction, which could lead to double handling if consumers listen to both.
No cleanup or unbinding path for removed/replaced DOM nodes; old listeners may remain if React re-renders discard bound elements.
Favour prompt accepts any input and converts with Number(), producing NaN for non-numeric values.
Throws an Error from decide() for non-canonical addresses, which will crash the calling handler unless wrapped.
notes: This is the middle of the gallery circuit: it turns rendered surfaces into address-keyed annotation targets and routes user marks outward as events, deliberately staying out of rendering and state persistence.
questions: What producer actually emits gallery:rendered / decision:rendered and what anchorableSelector or data-anchorable tagging does DNA provide?
Which downstream consumer handles gallery:verb versus the legacy gallery:direction, and when can the alias be removed?
How does the server-side decision_address canonicalization transform addresses, and does the JS shape guard match it exactly?
How are bound elements and their listeners handled when React re-renders or unmounts the mount container?
Where is the gallery icon set that replaces the inline ICONS map?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file binds rendered surfaces to address-keyed annotation/direction events without rendering or persisting state, and adds the decision-take path described in its comments.
apparent_themes: Event-driven decoupling between rendering, interaction, and state layers.
Address-centric identity for every annotatable surface element.
Contract discipline with explicit shape validation and fail-loud guards.
Transition compatibility between old and new event contracts.
Separation of concerns: this file neither renders nor writes state.
intention_signals: The file is trying to be a stable, portable interaction adapter that any gallery rendering implementation can drop in: it discovers addressable elements, attaches lightweight annotation controls, and forwards user direction to the owning state layer through a well-shaped event contract, while protecting decision writes from address-drift bugs.
novelty: The deliberate choice to validate but not canonicalize decision addresses in JavaScript, forcing non-canonical takes to fail loudly rather than risk a silent miss, and the dual legacy/new event emission as a documented transition alias.
contribution: A portable, address-keyed interaction binder that bridges rendered gallery surfaces and downstream consumers via custom events, with idempotent binding, explicit decision-address shape validation, and a transition alias for backward compatibility.
summary_for_embedding: wildcard-gallery-binder.js is a browser IIFE that listens for rendered-surface events, attaches comment/reaction/favour controls to addressable elements, and emits user direction as gallery:verb events while validating decision-card address shape.
intention_for_embedding: This file was intended to be the reusable middle layer of the gallery interaction circuit, converting rendered DOM into address-keyed annotation targets and routing user marks to the state layer without owning rendering or persistence.

symbols.does: Generates unique annotation identifiers using a local sequence counter and a window salt.
symbols.description: Combines an incrementing module-level _seq with a global window.__galleryAnnSalt to produce ids of the form ann-<seq>-<salt>, used to distinguish emitted comment, reaction, and favour marks.

symbols.does: Builds a comment mark object keyed to an element address.
symbols.description: Returns a canonical comment item shape with id, type 'comment', annotation_type, text, and element_id set to the supplied address, matching the server-side createComment shape referenced in the header.

symbols.does: Builds a reaction mark object keyed to an element address.
symbols.description: Returns a reaction item with id, type 'reaction', reaction value, and element_id set to the address, matching the server-side createReaction shape.

symbols.does: Builds a favour mark object keyed to an element address.
symbols.description: Returns a favour item with id, type 'favour', numeric score, and element_id set to the address, matching the server-side createFavour shape.

Wraps the verb, aim_address, and payload into a detail object and fires gallery:verb on window, intended as the single outbound contract for navigate, ask, annotate, drive, open-source, and generate lanes.

symbols.does: Routes an annotation item through the gallery:verb contract and re-emits the legacy gallery:direction shape.
symbols.description: Maps comment/reaction/favour fields onto the annotate verb payload, emits gallery:verb, then fires the older gallery:direction event with the raw item as a no-break transition alias.

symbols.does: Checks whether a decision address has the canonical decision://<frame>/<id> shape.
symbols.description: Validates that the address is a string starting with decision:// and follows one of the allowed frame patterns (global/<id>, project/<sid>/<id>, user/<sid>/<id>, session/<sid>/<id>), returning false for bare or malformed addresses.

symbols.does: Emits a decision_take mark after validating the decision address shape.
symbols.description: Throws a loud error and refuses to emit if the decision address is not canonical, otherwise dispatches gallery:verb with verb 'annotate', mark_type 'decision_take', the chosen option label as value, and an optional by field.

symbols.does: Attaches an annotation UI strip to a single rendered element keyed by address.
symbols.description: Marks the element as bound, sets data-element-id, injects a comment/favour/reaction strip, and wires click handlers that emit comment, favour, and reaction directions; DNA is expected to style the strip and placed stamps.

symbols.does: Renders a single-panel comment type picker and text input inside an element.
symbols.description: Creates a comment-picker with chips for the six content comment types, a textarea, and a send button; the chosen type and text are emitted as a comment direction when send is clicked.

symbols.does: Adds a visual placed-stamp element to a bound target after a reaction is chosen.
symbols.description: Appends a span with class placed-stamp and the reaction name, using the inline ICONS map or the reaction text, so DNA can style the resulting stamp.

symbols.does: Prompts the user for a favour score and returns it as a number.
symbols.description: Uses window.prompt to ask for a numeric favour score and returns Number(promptValue), returning null if the user cancels.

symbols.does: Handles gallery:rendered and decision:rendered events to bind annotatable elements.
symbols.description: Reads the event detail, binds explicit element lists if provided, otherwise walks the supplied root container using an anchorable selector (DNA-declared, data-anchorable, or semantic fallback), derives sub-addresses, calls makeAnnotatable for each, and warns if zero elements are bound.
